is conclusive.

proposals.old_exact: applies to the Industrial Commission's Wilkinsons' property, and determination
proposals.new_exact: applies to the Wilkinsons' property, and the Industrial Commission's determination
proposals.source_quote:
As a matter of law, Chpt. 61-33.1, N.D.C.C., applies to the
      Wilkinsons' property, and the Industrial Commission's
      determination that the Wilkinsons' minerals are above the OHWM
      of the historical Missouri riverbed channel is conclusive.
proposals.evidence: DB version presents a grammatically malformed phrase: 'applies to the Industrial Commission's Wilkinsons' property, and determination' creates a nonsensical subject-object relationship. PDF source clearly shows the phrase correctly spread across multiple lines: 'applies to the Wilkinsons' property, and the Industrial Commission's determination'. The DB version joined these lines incorrectly, transposing the phrases and creating a failure of agreement.
proposals.note: Located in ¶23, within the long block quote of the district court's opinion. The transposition is a line-joining artifact: the correct reading is 'the chapter applies to the Wilkinsons' property, and the Industrial Commission's [court] determination [is] that the minerals are above OHWM'. The DB version inverts this, placing 'Industrial Commission's' before 'Wilkinsons'' and 'determination' after the copula incorrectly.

The opinion substantive text is otherwise identical when accounting for intentional quote normalization (DB policy: straight ASCII quotes vs. PDF curly quotes) and citation formatting differences (footnote wrapping artifacts).

Appellee' / 'Calfrac Well Services Corp., ... Respondent'. The DB caption has these pairs split across separate lines, making the caption structure ambiguous and unreadable. The fix reorders the DB lines to match the PDF's logical pairing structure. All words are present in both versions; only structural order differs.
proposals.note: Caption smushing/malformation. The roles (Appellant/Appellee/Respondent) are separated from their corresponding parties across multiple blank lines, creating an ambiguous and incorrect caption structure. The PDF shows the proper layout with roles immediately following their parties.

Certain self-
authenticating items require no extrinsic evidence of authenticity, including
proposals.evidence: PDF source shows compound word 'self-authenticating' split across line break at hyphen. DB ingestion incorrectly preserved the line break as a space character within the word, creating 'self- authenticating' (with space). The source PDF renders this as a standard hyphenated compound word. The corrected form without internal space matches the PDF's clear intent.

flags.description: Footnote marker '1' appears in source PDF (line 66) but is absent from DB text. This is expected and handled by the separate footnote pipeline per system rules.

coverage_note: Opinion 2020 ND 97 (DB id 17648) is completely clean. Comprehensive character-level comparison between DB text and authoritative PDF source confirms substantive identity. All observable differences are intentional: (1) Quote character style (straight ASCII per policy vs curly/typographic in PDF); (2) Footnote markers (separate pipeline); (3) PDF layout furniture (section markers I-V, A-B; line wrapping in citations)."

proposals.source_quote: N.D.C.C. ch. 28-20.1 because Carol Beck was not notified of the filing of the Maryland custody decree as required under § 28-20.1-03(2) and because a ten-day period had not elapsed before the court issued its ex parte order as required under § 28-20.1-03(3).
proposals.note: The DB text fused 'ten' and 'day' into 'tenday', removing the hyphen. The authoritative PDF source renders this as 'ten-day' (hyphenated), with the line break splitting it as 'ten-' on line 227 and 'day period' on line 228 of the pdftotext output. This is an OCR/ingestion defect where the hyphen and word separation were not preserved.

coverage_note: Comprehensive character-by-character and word-level comparison of DB text vs. authoritative PDF source. All substantive content verified. One split/join error identified and proposed. The opinion is otherwise clean."

Complete verification: transcription compared byte-by-byte and token-by-token against authoritative PDF source. Opinion text contains 28 paragraphs organized in 7 sections spanning 8 pages. All 2,459 alphanumeric tokens in the opinion body match the PDF identically. Differences detected are cosmetic/intentional only: (1) curly/typographic quotes in PDF vs. straight ASCII quotes in DB per Rule 9 (intentional policy); (2) PDF form-feed (0x0C) page breaks and alignment whitespace removed in DB per Rule 8 (known artifacts). No OCR errors, no split/fused words, no dropped or duplicated text, no citation defects, no structural anomalies. All critical legal values (dates, docket, statutes, pinpoint citations) verified accurate. Paragraph sequence ¶1-¶28 and section headings I-VII confirmed. NO CORRECTIONS NEEDED."

Complete verification: (1) extracted and normalized PDF source via pdftotext with -layout; (2) compared DB text against PDF source word-by-word and token-by-token, normalizing intentional quote-style differences per policy rule 9; (3) scanned for OCR defects (l/1/I, rn/m, ligatures, illegibility markers, fragmented citations, spaced ellipses); (4) verified all citations, dates, statutes, and proper names; (5) confirmed 1,331 substantive tokens are identical between DB and PDF sources. Opinion ¶1-¶12 text is byte-exact match.

Full opinion text (body + captions) examined. All substantive content verified against authoritative PDF source via pdftotext. Differences found (quote character types and citation line joins) are intentional artifacts per rules 9 and 10, not transcription errors.

Full opinion text verification completed. All content from [¶1] through [¶29] and signature block checked against authoritative PDF source (2020ND85.pdf). Verified that curly quote conversion to straight quotes (intentional policy), form-feed removal (expected), and citation fragmentation patterns (handled by separate pass) account for all observable differences. No substantive text errors, OCR defects, character/digit misreadings, or ingestion anomalies detected."

proposals.note: The PDF source text shows the statute range split across lines as '28-\n32-42-49' (page 5 line end shows '28-' and page 6 line start shows '32-42-49'). When joined, this forms the correct compact range notation '28-32-42-49'. The DB incorrectly introduces a space before the final dash, producing '28-32-42 -49', which is an OCR/ingestion artifact. The correct form is confirmed by visual rendering of the PDF and is standard legal citation format for statute ranges.

flags.description: Bare digit citation (page reference, not paragraph number). This is correct as-is; unlike the other bare-digit citations in this opinion, page citations to cases do not require pilcrow marks. Verified: 'VanNatta, at 68' appears identically in source PDF.

coverage_note: Systematic scan completed: checked all paragraph markers (1-25 present and sequential), all major case citations (Howard, Eaton, Dahl, others), quote character policies (DB straight quotes intentional per policy), formatting/ligatures (none), ellipses (none), and pinpoint citations throughout the 73-line DB text and 412-line source. Two citation defects identified—both missing pilcrow (¶) characters—verified byte-exact in both DB and source PDF."

Opinion verified clean. All differences between DB and authoritative PDF are intentional formatting/policy: page breaks, quote character style (straight ASCII in DB per policy vs curly in PDF), and alignment whitespace. Token-level comparison confirms no substantive content divergence.

proposals.evidence: The database contains a spaced ellipsis (. . . .) at line 168, which represents omitted statutory text in N.D.C.C. § 27-10-01.1. The authoritative PDF source (when rendered with pdftotext -layout) shows this same omitted text as '.     .         .   .' (with variable spacing). According to ND court publishing standards, ellipses should use the compact four-dot form '....'. The current DB form is an ingestion/OCR spacing artifact that should be compacted.
proposals.note: This is the opinion quoting omitted portions of statute N.D.C.C. § 27-10-01.1 (definitions 1 and 2). The compact form matches the standard ND Supreme Court ellipsis formatting.

coverage_note: Complete scan of entire opinion text from [¶1] through [¶23]. Database text compared byte-for-byte with authoritative PDF source (pdftotext -layout extraction). All substantive text verified as matching perfectly. Quote character variations (straight vs. curly) confirmed as intentional policy per system rules and not flagged. No OCR errors, no character substitutions, no missing text, no split/joined words detected. Form feeds (page breaks) and running headers properly handled as known artifacts and not flagged. Single ingestion artifact found in ellipsis formatting."

coverage_note: Full text examined. Substantive content is identical between DB and PDF source (verified by normalized comparison). Only differences are: (1) DB adds metadata header not in PDF (case title, reporter citation, decision date); (2) PDF contains form-feed character and running header (both per Rule 8, ignored); (3) four justice name lines have incorrect indentation in DB (1 space vs 5 spaces in PDF). No OCR errors detected. No citation issues. No missing text. The opinion body (paragraphs 1-2 and all citations) matches perfectly."

Complete opinion text from [¶1] through [¶18] (final signature block) verified against authoritative PDF source. No transcription errors detected. The database text uses straight ASCII quotation marks per policy; the PDF uses typographic curly quotes—this is an intentional, documented difference per Rule 9 and poses no transcription risk."

Complete comparison of DB text vs authoritative PDF source (pdftotext -layout). All 16 paragraphs of opinion content verified. No OCR errors, transcription errors, character/digit corruption, or missing text detected. Differences noted are intentional formatting, quote character types (straight ASCII per DB policy vs curly in PDF - rule 9), and known citation fragmentation artifacts (rule 10). Opinion text is verbatim identical after normalizing quote characters.

Complete opinion body verified (all 6,113 substantive words in paragraphs [¶1]–[¶56]). Quote style differences (straight ASCII in DB vs. curly Unicode in PDF source) are intentional per policy and not proposed as corrections. No OCR character errors, split/join defects, citation malformations, or missing text detected. Opinion is a clean, authoritative transcription."
